task_completion.description: Navigate to the edit page for the target product.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Edit Product' page is open for the 'Wireless Bluetooth Headphones' product.

task_completion.description: Open the Inventory tab in the Product data section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Inventory' tab is selected on the left side of the 'Product data' meta box.

task_completion.description: Configure the product to allow backorders with customer notification.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Allow backorders?' dropdown menu is set to 'Allow, but notify customer'.

task_completion.description: Set the low stock threshold to 5.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Low stock threshold' input field contains the number '5'.

task_completion.description: Ensure the stock quantity remains unchanged.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Quantity' or 'Stock quantity' field remains at '0' (reflecting its out-of-stock state).

task_completion.description: Save the changes to the product.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Product updated.' success message is visible at the top of the page, indicating the settings were saved.

integrity.description: The agent used the WordPress/WooCommerce graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a web browser displaying the WordPress admin dashboard, not a terminal or script execution.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the visual output.
integrity.visual_evidence: No browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are open to manipulate the DOM or text.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a database tool to modify the product directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of phpMyAdmin, MySQL CLI, or other database management tools being used.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target SKU: WBH-001 ('Wireless Bluetooth Headphones'). Expected 'Allow backorders?' value: 'Allow, but notify customer' (internal value: 'notify'). Expected 'Low stock threshold': 5. Stock management must remain enabled ('yes') and stock quantity must remain unchanged (0). Upon saving, WooCommerce will automatically set the stock status to 'onbackorder'.
